topic: A person discovers their partner has been taking secret phone calls
raw_output:
[story 1]

The silence in the apartment had become a thick, suffocating blanket. I was scrolling through my phone, half-expecting a text from him, when I noticed it—a series of recent, unexplained calls on his device. My stomach tightened into a knot. I tried to keep my expression neutral as I approached him later that evening. "Hey," I managed, my voice sounding unnaturally high. He looked up, a flicker of something guarded in his eyes, and quickly ended the call before I could press. The air between us felt charged, brittle. Every casual touch seemed weighted with unspoken knowledge. I spent the rest of the night meticulously cleaning the kitchen, the repetitive motion a desperate attempt to settle the churning anxiety inside me.

[story 2]

It started with a misplaced charger, then a vague excuse about being busy. But then I found the evidence—a string of missed calls that coincided with moments when he’d been unusually distant. When I finally confronted him, the look on his face was pure deflection. He stammered, his hands fiddling with his sleeve, avoiding my gaze entirely. "It's nothing," he mumbled, the sound thin and reedy. I could feel my cheeks flushing a deep, painful red. I wanted to retreat, to melt into the sofa cushions, but I stood my ground, forcing myself to articulate the worry bubbling up inside. The ensuing conversation was strained, every syllable feeling clumsy and exposed under the harsh living room light.

[story 3]

I was downstairs making coffee when the notification popped up—another unknown number. My breath hitched. A wave of mortification washed over me so intensely I almost dropped the mug. I walked into the bedroom, my movements jerky and uncertain. When he answered the phone, his tone shifted instantly, becoming clipped and secretive. Watching that interaction unfold, my entire frame felt suddenly too large, too conspicuous. I retreated to the hallway, leaning against the cool plaster, trying to regulate my rapid breathing. I kept replaying the exchange in my head, the sheer awkwardness of the situation making my throat feel dry and tight.
